app.tagline: Japanese the way it's spoken — 55% polite, 45% colloquial

home.testReadyTitle: Week done — ready for the test? 🏯
home.testReadySub: You hit 100% of week {{week}}'s goal. Pass the (rigorous) test to unlock the next week.
home.lessonsSub: Read these before the Build Sentences exercise — guessing rarely works. Each one takes just a couple minutes.

ctaTest.title: Ready for the week test?
ctaTest.sub: You hit 100% of the goal. Pass (100% required) to unlock the next week.

ctaChat.title: Chat with a friend
ctaChat.sub: One chat today keeps your register sharp.

ctaDone.title: Daily goal hit! 🌸
ctaDone.sub: Anything past this is bonus. How about a casual chat?

home.goalHintShort: Finish the tracks to stay on par with the week.
home.freePace: Progress is free — this is just the suggested pace to finish the 20 weeks.
home.behind: No rush — the pace is yours. 🍵
home.goalHint: Finish today's tracks to stay on par with this week's curriculum.
home.regHint: Based on the chats you've finished — the more you talk, the more this reflects your real balance.
home.goalDone: Daily goal complete! 🎉 Anything more is bonus.

curriculum.title: 20-week path
curriculum.subtitle: Everything is unlocked from day one. The path marks the suggested pace, not a lock.
curriculum.comingSoon: This week's content is in production — the engine is ready for it.
curriculum.subtitleMastery: You advance by mastery, not by calendar. Pass a week's test to unlock the next.
curriculum.lockedByMastery: Pass the previous week's test to unlock.

test.title: Week {{week}} test 🏯
test.rule1: {{n}} questions — mixes hiragana, katakana, sentence building and register choice.
test.rule2: Miss one? Restart. Rigor is what validates the advance.
test.rule3: No time limit, no penalty — you can retry as much as you want.
test.failedSub: You missed {{n}} of {{total}}. House rule: passing needs 100%.
test.failedHint: A quick 5-minute review before retrying usually does it. The test is rebuilt from scratch with fresh shuffling every time.

numbers.title: Numbers and money
numbers.subtitle: Where 90% of tourists freeze. Prices, times and counters — until they're automatic.
numbers.hearPrompt: Listen to the price and type it in digits:
numbers.micMiss: Didn't match — check the answer and retry.
numbers.timeHint: 💡 30 minutes = はん (half). 4:00 = よじ, 9:00 = くじ — the irregular hours!
numbers.counterHint: 💡 Counters change with what you count: people, cups, tickets, bottles…

scenario.sayIt: Say this reply into the mic
scenario.micMiss: Heard 「{{heard}}」 — try again or tap the option.

slot.sectionTitle: Pick the words — the chat flows
slot.doneSub: Every reply came from YOUR head. That's real conversation.

speak.off: Hmm, that came out different. Listen again and repeat.
speak.silent: Didn't catch anything — get closer to the mic.
speak.micError: Couldn't access the microphone. Check the browser permission.
speak.hint: Tip: speak at a natural pace, without pausing between syllables.
speak.unsupported: Your browser doesn't support speech recognition
speak.unsupportedSub: Use Chrome or Brave to practice pronunciation. Everything else works normally.

sentence.tapHint: Tap the tiles in the right order
sentence.wrong: Almost! The order isn't right. Try again.
sentence.emptySub: This content is on the way.

flash.dailyDoneSub: That's the suggested pace so memory sticks without overload. But progress is free — you can keep going.
flash.allLearned: You've seen every hiragana! Come back later for reviews, or practice now.

trace.title: Writing practice
trace.instructions: Trace the character over the model. Mind the stroke order!
trace.attribution: Strokes: KanjiVG © Ulrich Apel, CC BY-SA 3.0

dialogue.title: Chats
dialogue.subtitle: Talk with your Japanese friends — each one lives in a different register. Chats follow your level.

onboarding.title: Welcome to KawaiiLearn!
onboarding.subtitle: Two quick questions to make the app yours.
onboarding.genderQ: How should the app refer to you?
onboarding.pronounNote: In Japanese, 'I' changes with the speaker: 私 (watashi) is universal, 僕 (boku) is casual masculine, あたし (atashi) is casual feminine. Your pick becomes a hint in lessons!
onboarding.crush_haruto: The cute barista guy who memorized your order
onboarding.crush_mei: The cute barista girl who memorized your order

yuki.bio: 22, infinite energy, speaks 100% casual and loves correcting you while laughing.

haruto.bio: Works at the café by the station. Started polite, but he's getting casual with you…

mei.bio: Works at the café by the station. Started polite, but she's getting casual with you…

kenji.bio: Always courteous, always in です・ます. Perfect for training the polite register.

hana.bio: An obaachan who gives you tangerines and speaks warm, unhurried polite Japanese.

aiko.bio: Explains everything patiently and shows you when to use each register.

notif.title: Let your friends reach out 🌸
notif.sub: Yuki, Kenji, Hana, Aiko and your crush can drop a line during the day — each at the time that fits them.
notif.rule1: At most 2 messages a day — no spam.
notif.rule2: Nothing between 10pm and 7am — respects your sleep.
notif.rule3: Every character only pings you at their in-character hours.
notif.denied: Notifications are blocked in your browser settings. Reopen the permission for KawaiiLearn and come back.
notif.onSub: Your friends will reach out at natural hours. To turn off, use your browser settings.
